cityDNA.coreFantasy: Thalassmere is a merfolk harbor-basin city where canals, cargo law, public works, nurseries, and sacred harbor music organize tidebound life.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Thalassmere should feel immediately distinct: merfolk civic life is organized around Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, and Harbor Campus, with Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir as the citywide symbol.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The city turns Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow, Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, and Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Tide Sorcery into a working urban system rather than surface decoration.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Use Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, S1 Canal Hall, Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Public Works Authority Hall, visible Canal Hall Beacon, the central Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, and the skyline-scale Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir as the core visual grammar.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A merfolk population whose institutions make daily life legible through section anchors: Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Harbor Campus.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city is symbolized by Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir, Canal Hall Beacon, Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel, and the repeating civic pattern of hall, landmark, monument, and nook in every section.

canonLocks: Thalassmere is built for its merfolk population and should not be genericized into a neutral fantasy city.
The Wonder is Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir.
The Beacon is Canal Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five section spines are Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Harbor Campus.
The tactical board uses 25 district slots, with District 13 as the seat-of-power center.
Exact sidecar names for civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks must be preserved verbatim.
provisionalCanon: Citizens likely treat Canal Hall Beacon as the practical public signal point for citywide alerts and Conjunction response.
Daily politics likely moves through the named section institutions rather than through faction headquarters.
The city’s civic magic expresses through Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Tide Sorcery when visible in public spaces.
rejectedReads: Thalassmere as a generic fantasy settlement with the culture applied as ornament only.
Replacing exact section anchors with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, archive, or court when the sidecar provides a proper name.
Treating the Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power as interchangeable skyline props instead of civic machinery.
cautionFlags: Do not add unsupported per-district landmark names beyond the source-backed section anchors.
Keep all 25 districts, but derive their specific identity from the five section spines unless new district-specific sidecars exist.
Preserve the material language: Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow.
Preserve the terrain/water logic: Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast.

environment.environmentalLogic: Environment should make the city’s institutions readable: Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Harbor Campus must feel embedded in Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin, with water handled as Coast and public magic appearing as Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Tide Sorcery.

skyline: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir dominates the far skyline and should be larger than every section landmark.
Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel anchors the central District 13 civic mass.
Canal Hall Beacon is the section-1 signal landmark and must be readable from nearby districts.
Section skyline rhythm: Canal Hall Beacon, Cargo Yard, Survey Hall, Nursery Basin, Harbor Basilica.

civilization.people: The population is merfolk. Civic scenes should show them using the city’s architecture naturally: gathering near Canal Hall Beacon, moving through Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, and treating the named anchors as practical institutions.
socialClasses: seat-of-power officials attached to Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel
section hall administrators and permit keepers
craft, market, transport, religious, or military guild households depending on district spine
street vendors, carriers, maintenance workers, and household service families
visitors and petitioners who navigate the city through public anchors
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel and expressed locally through the five section civic halls. Authority should be visible in courts, archives, public signals, permits, and named monuments rather than hidden abstraction.
civilization.religion: Religious and symbolic life should draw on Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Tide Sorcery, the Wonder’s public symbolism, and whichever section anchors carry sacred or ceremonial roles.
customs: citizens use named halls and nooks as practical meeting points
public signals from the Beacon interrupt ordinary commerce and movement
section monuments preserve civic memory and local identity
visitors orient themselves by Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section major landmarks
formal petitions and judgments move toward District 13
bodyLanguage: citizens make room for official messengers near beacon approaches
locals gesture by landmark rather than by street name
public posture changes around courts, monuments, and halls
market or service workers keep one eye on signal points during tense periods
speechPatterns: street speech abbreviates section landmarks but not Wonder, Beacon, or Seat names
public announcements use practical civic language rather than poetic vagueness

streets.appearance: Street views should show Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, S1 Canal Hall, pedestrians using section halls, and sightlines back to Canal Hall Beacon or Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel where appropriate.
streets.movement: Movement is organized by section spine: Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Harbor Campus. Districts feed toward their civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook before converging on District 13 for authority scenes.
streets.lighting: Use civic blue illumination for official signal moments, practical lantern light for night scenes, and material-specific highlights on Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce terrain and water logic: Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context Coast.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings emphasize deliveries, hall openings, street maintenance, market preparation, and official notices; afternoons build toward hearings, training, guild work, or section-specific labor.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings compress around lanterns, food stalls, guarded thresholds, quieter civic errands, and beacon visibility against darker streets.

prestigious: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel document ateliers
Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir ceremonial suppliers

housing.wealthy: Residences near Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel or high-status section courts use the best Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, private terraces, and controlled views of civic landmarks.
housing.middle: Households cluster above shops and along civic streets, sharing courtyards, balconies, workrooms, and access to section halls.
housing.poor: Lower-status housing is practical, crowded, and close to service yards or labor streets, but still uses local motifs and public landmarks for orientation.

wonder.name: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of merfolk identity made public and monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A one-per-city wonder: spectacular, unmistakable, and larger than every section landmark. It turns Thalassmere's geography, race identity, material culture, and civic magic into one public symbol. Built for Thalassmere's merfolk population, it uses Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast; magic expression: Ward Networ
wonder.civicRole: A unifying civic symbol larger than section landmarks; it gives Thalassmere its skyline identity and public myth.
storyHooks: a public ceremony at the Wonder changes city mood
maintenance access reveals hidden civic routes
the Wonder becomes the reference point during a beacon state

beacon.name: Canal Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: The public signal point where citywide warning becomes local action.
beacon.visualRole: Canal Hall Beacon: the single city beacon, a towering civic signal landmark fixed to section 1. Visible signal apparatus, strong vertical silhouette, public approach space, and board-scale beacon identity.
beacon.civicRole: The Section 1 signal landmark; citizens watch it for alerts, public notices, and Conjunction response.
storyHooks: a signal is interpreted before officials arrive
crowds block the approach
the beacon reveals which district reacts first

seatOfPower.name: Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Civic authority made visible through courts, archives, command platforms, and public legitimacy.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. It is not a faction HQ; it is the city's central civic authority. Built for Thalassmere's merfolk population, it uses Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin; water context: Coast; magic expression: Ward Ne
seatOfPower.civicRole: District 13 authority center for petitions, archives, judgments, and city-scale decisions; not a faction headquarters.
storyHooks: a sealed record contradicts public belief
a petition exposes citywide risk
command platforms become crowd-facing stages

majorLandmarks.name: S1 Canal Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Section 1 civic center preserving the local identity of Canal.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Canal Hall: authored civic gathering structure for section 1. institutional anchor. public-facing administration. clear central silhouette. board-readable entrance. water gauges. sluice wheels. reeds. wet stone. lock houses. sluice gates. waterwheel yards. towpaths
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Supports Canal as a playable and writable district spine rather than a generic neighborhood.
storyHooks: an event at S1 Canal Hall reveals section priorities
locals use S1 Canal Hall as shorthand for the district
a small failure here cascades into a citywide problem

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 / Canal must preserve S1 Canal Hall, Canal Hall Beacon, S1 Canal Pillar, and S1 Canal Kiosk as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S2 / Warehousing / Logistics must preserve Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, and Inventory Hall Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S3 / Public Works Authority must preserve Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, and Permit Yard Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S4 / Nursery / Spawning Grounds must preserve Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, and Spawning Pool Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S5 / Harbor Campus must preserve S5 Harbor Campus, Harbor Basilica, S5 Harbor Arch, and S5 Harbor Bridgelet as the source-backed anchor cluster.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Thalassmere as a merfolk city using Pale Marble Stone, Coral Stone, Seashell, Mother of Pearl, Arcane Glow across Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin.
Keep Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir as the skyline-scale Wonder, Shell Spiral Windows Tide-Seal Citadel as central D13 authority, and Canal Hall Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Use section landmark distribution to avoid flattening the city: Canal, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Harbor Campus.
Water and terrain must follow source context: Coast; Coastal Edge, Seawall Edge, Harbor Basin.
requiredVisualElements: Merfolk Coral Lyre Basin of the Deep Choir
elementsToAvoid: invented district-specific landmarks not supported by sidecars
replacing proper anchor names with generic summaries
collapsing five section spines into one repeated street type
